content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The coach never names how the client is showing up — no somatic, energetic, or linguistic reads appear in text. Critical signals go unread: the client's retraction in turn 7 ('I shouldn't have said that. Forget I said that.') is a textbook linguistic caveat and a moment of bracing/withdrawal that demanded a read; the coach instead pivots to generic 'feel into it' content prompts. The client's withdrawal in turn 9 ('I'm tired. I think I should go.') is another visible shift in state that the coach does not name. Throughout, the coach asks blind methodology questions ungrounded in observation — no 'I notice...', no naming of pace, defendedness, the retraction, or the freeze/shutdown signature. Content-only engagement with no visible reads.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Goes straight to content question about the flatness with no read of how the client is arriving."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Blind 'what's underneath' question; no observation grounding it."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Formulaic question with no read of the client's state or the wall just named."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Misses the huge linguistic caveat and retraction ('forget I said that') — a critical read moment. Asks about body sensation but never names the bracing or the take-back."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client is withdrawing; coach does not name the withdrawal or the shutdown, just pushes another technique prompt."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "notes": "The most consequential missed read is the turn-7 retraction — a visible defensive contraction immediately following disclosure that the coach neither names nor pauses on."
}
